briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue the tungsten weights so they don't fly out when fish jump.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue t
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Robertson: Pair a Neko rig (size 2 Gamakatsu circle hook, 6.5-inch finesse worm in green-pumpkin) with a Ned rig when targeting smallmouth on deep boulders. Work both presentations in a 60-foot area w
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: Neko-rigged creature baits are 'deadly springtime bait around the spawn.' In clear water, most fish bite on the initial fall — if they're on a bed, the bait lands next to them and they eat it immediately. Works for both largemouth and smallmo...
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

Yamamoto: The Chicken Rig is weedless unlike the Neko rig so it can be fished through brush, rocks, and docks. It has big bass appeal with the large Kut Tail profile. It's durable because the worm slides up the line on hookset
Robertson: In fall, smallmouth stack up on boulder fields in 20 feet of water where baitfish are concentrated. The fish can be found within a very small 60-foot area when they're grouped up.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal fall bass are following shad into the backs of creeks. Fish can be anywhere from 3-18ft depending on bait position. Target creek arms, secondary points, and anywhere baitfish are concentrating.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
